title: heliophila cornuta var. squamata
intro: Heliophila cornuta var. squamata, commonly known as the hairy heliophila, is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema. The health benefits of the hairy heliophila include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and protect against infection. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusing its leaves in water or oil to create a topical treatment for skin conditions, and in some cases, it is also used as a food ingredient in traditional cuisine.
